standard: California Consumer Privacy Act (Cal. Civ. Code §1798.100–1798.199) + CPRA Amendments
notes: 15 CCPA/CPRA obligations as auditable data checks. Includes CPRA (Prop 24) amendments effective 2023-01-01. v0.1.0 - baseline model. Not legal advice. Validate against current CPPA regulations before use in a compliance engagement.

obligations.title: Right to Know — Consumer Identity Linkable
obligations.description: Consumers have the right to request disclosure of personal information collected about them. Records must be linkable to a consumer identity to fulfill access requests.

obligations.title: Right to Delete — Deletion Request Mechanism
obligations.description: Consumers have the right to request deletion of personal information collected from them. A mechanism to record, track, and execute deletion requests must exist.

obligations.title: Right to Correct — Correction Request Tracking (CPRA)
obligations.description: CPRA amendment: Consumers have the right to correct inaccurate personal information. Correction requests must be recorded and actioned.

obligations.title: Right to Know — Categories of Personal Information
obligations.description: Consumers may request the categories of personal information collected, the purposes for collection, and categories of third parties with whom it is shared.

obligations.title: Right to Know — Sale and Disclosure Tracking
obligations.description: Consumers have the right to know what categories of personal information are sold or disclosed to third parties for business purposes.

obligations.title: Right to Opt-Out of Sale
obligations.description: Consumers have the right to direct a business not to sell their personal information. Opt-out status and date must be recorded per consumer.

obligations.title: Right to Opt-Out of Sharing (CPRA)
obligations.description: CPRA amendment: Extends opt-out rights to cover sharing of personal information for cross-context behavioral advertising, not just sale.

obligations.title: Right to Limit Sensitive PI Use (CPRA)
obligations.description: CPRA amendment: Consumers have the right to limit the use and disclosure of sensitive personal information (SPI) to necessary purposes. SPI must be flagged and request mechanism must exist.

obligations.title: Right to Non-Discrimination
obligations.description: Businesses may not discriminate against consumers for exercising CCPA rights (no denial of service, different pricing, or different quality). Opt-out consumers must not receive degraded service records.
obligations.notes: Rell flags consumer records where service tier, price, or access level differs after opt-out or deletion request. Cross-reference opt_out_sale_flag / deletion_requested against service_tier or pricing_tier if present.

obligations.title: Notice at Collection
obligations.description: Businesses must inform consumers at or before the point of collection about the categories of personal information collected and the purposes for which it will be used.

obligations.title: Opt-Out Mechanism — Do Not Sell or Share Link
obligations.description: Businesses that sell or share personal information must provide a clear and conspicuous 'Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information' link or equivalent mechanism.
obligations.notes: Rell flags consumer-facing datasets from systems with no documented opt-out mechanism reference. Check for opt_out_mechanism_url or opt_out_presented_flag in schema. Absence is a HIGH finding.

obligations.title: Sensitive Personal Information Classification (CPRA)
obligations.description: CPRA defines a new category of Sensitive Personal Information (SPI) including SSNs, financial account data, health data, biometrics, geolocation, and more. SPI must be identified and classified in the data schema.

obligations.title: Security — Breach Exposure Tracking
obligations.description: Consumers have a private right of action if their non-encrypted, non-redacted personal information is subject to unauthorized access due to a business's failure to implement reasonable security. Breach incidents must be recorded.

obligations.title: Consumer Request Verification and Response
obligations.description: Businesses must establish a process to verify consumer identity for requests and respond within 45 days (extendable to 90 days). Request intake, verification status, and response date must be tracked.
obligations.notes: Rell flags any consumer request record where the gap between request_received_date and request_response_date exceeds 45 days without an extension flag.

obligations.title: Retention Period Disclosure (CPRA)
obligations.description: CPRA amendment: Businesses must disclose the length of time they intend to retain each category of personal information, or the criteria used to determine that period.
